data.title: India's defence stocks gain amid India-Pakistan tensions
data.description:
** India's defence stocks (.NIFTYINDDEFENCE) up 1.6%, taking gains to 0.6% in two sessions since India attacked Pakistan early Wednesday

** Defence stocks up 3.8% since April 22 attack in Indian Kashmir, while benchmark Nifty 50 is up 1%

** The tensions may lead to increased defence spending through the year, says Bernstein

** Defence companies' order books to get larger, pace of execution to increase, possibly boosting revenues and earnings forecast, says Vikas Gupta, CEO and chief investment strategist at OmniScience Capital

** On the day, Paras Defence and Space Technologies and Bharat Dynamics gain about 2.5% each

** Bharat Electronics and Hindustan Aeronautics up 1.1% and 1.4%, respectively

data.title: India's Voltas rises on Q4 profit jump
data.description:
** Shares of air conditioner maker Voltas up 0.5%, after rising as much as 3.5% earlier

** Co reports Q4 net profit of 2.41 bln rupees ($28.4 mln), up more than 100% y/y

** Rev up 13.4% to 47.68 bln rupees, helped by a 17% rise in unitary cooling products segment

** Stock on track to snap 2 weeks of losses; gained 3% so far this week

** More than 4.8 mln shares traded as of 12:44 p.m. IST, 2.4x their 30-day moving avg

** Mean rating of stock is 'buy'; their median PT is 1,520 rupees - data compiled by LSEG

** Stock trims YTD losses to 30.5%

($1 = 84.8900 Indian rupees)

data.title: Gulf Cement Flags Trading Halt on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Gulf Cement Co. (ADX:GCEM) will halt trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 2 pm local time on May 13 due to a board meeting.

The Abu Dhabi-listed cement manufacturing company's shares will restart trading after it publishes the results of its meeting, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Hikma Pharmaceuticals: Settlement Not Admission of Wrongdoing or Liability
Hikma Pharmaceuticals Will Continue to Defend Itself Against Any Other Litigation
Serbia's industrial PPI up 0.1% y/y in April - table
data.description:
BELGRADE (Serbia), May 8 (SeeNews) - Serbia's industrial producer prices edged up by 0.1% on the year in April, slowing down from the 0.6% annual growth in the prior month, the country's statistical office said.

In April, producer prices in the mining sector fell by an annual 0.2% and prices in manufacturing were also down 0.2% compared to the same month last year. At the same time, electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply producer prices rose by an annual 1.1%, and prices in water supply jumped 20.9% on the year, the statistical office said in a data release earlier this week.

On a monthly comparison basis, overall producer prices declined by 0.2% in April, following a 0.1% drop in March. In particular, producer prices in the mining sector fell by a monthly 3.4% in April, and manufacturing prices dropped 0.2%, whereas the cost of electricity, gas, steam and air conditioning supply remained flat compared to March, and producer prices in water supply rose by 0.3%.

In January-April, overall producer prices were up 1.1% compared to the same period of 2024.

In 2024, Serbia's industrial producer prices inched up by 0.7% after advancing by 3.3% in 2023.

Following are percentage changes in Serbia's total industrial PPI, breakdown by sector:

data.title: Scatec gains after outlook hike
data.description:
** Shares in Scatec rise 6% after the Norwegian renewable energy solutions provider reported Q1 beat and hiked its full-year earnings outlook

** Scatec increased its proportionate EBITDA outlook by NOK 400 million to NOK 4.15-4.45 billion ($830.7 million)

** "Beat is from operations in the Philippines doing markedly better than expected," Nordea analyst Andreas Nibe Nygard says

** If gains hold, shares will see their best day since August 2024

($1 = 10.3524 Norwegian crowns)

data.title: AIB Group Plans to Launch $1.36 Billion Buyback
data.description:
By Najat Kantouar

AIB Group agreed with Ireland's finance department to launch a 1.2 billion euros buyback program ($1.36 billion).

The Irish lender on Thursday said that it agreed to make an off-market purchase of around 191.7 million ordinary shares at 6.2607 euros a share. This represents about 8.2% of the company's issued share capital.

Following the completion of transaction, the finance department will hold around 271.2 million warrants to subscribe for ordinary shares, representing approximately 12.7% of the company's issued share capital.

The buyback contract was approved by the company's shareholders at the annual general meeting, and the off-market purchase is expected to settle on May 9.

Write to Najat Kantouar at najat.kantouar@wsj.com

data.title: Xianju Pharmaceutical Receives Acceptance Notice for Drug Consistency Evaluation
data.description:
Zhejiang Xianju Pharmaceutical received an acceptance notice from China's drug administrator for the consistency evaluation of its ethinyl estradiol cyproterone tablets.

The drug is used to treat androgen-dependent conditions in women such as acne, alopecia, and hirsutism, according to a Thursday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The drug company said it will actively promote the subsequent related work.

Shares of Xianju Pharmaceutical closed 2% higher Thursday.

data.title: Metro Bank's Profit Rose in First Quarter
data.description:
By Elena Vardon

Metro Bank Holdings said it was profitable in the first quarter and is confident that it will meet its guidance for the year.

The U.K. high-street lender on Thursday said that its profit for the three months ended March 31 rose significantly compared with the second half of 2024, for which it reported 12.8 million pounds ($17.0 million) in pretax profit. While it didn't provide any figures, it attributed the improvement to a structurally higher net interest margin from asset rotation and deposit optimization, along with continued cost reduction.

The bank reported 8.465 billion pounds in loans for the period, down 28% on year due to the disposal of its unsecured personal loan portfolio but highlighted growth in corporate and commercial lending--an area of strategic focus.

Customer deposits fell 15% to 13.82 billion pounds as higher-cost fixed-term deposits matured and it focused on reducing excess liquidity and costs, it added.

"During the first quarter of 2025, we have continued to deliver the strategic repositioning of Metro Bank's business," Chief Executive Daniel Frumkin said.

For 2025, Metro Bank has guided for its return on tangible equity--a key profitability metric--to land in the mid-to-upper single-digits. It expects the net interest margin for the year to be between 3.00% and 3.25% and to reduce costs by 4% to 5%.

Shares edged up 0.8% to 105 pence at market open.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: Invitation to the Presentation of Albert's Interim Report for January-March 2025
data.description:
eEducation Albert AB (publ) will publish its interim report for January-March 2025 on 16 May at 07:30. On the same day at 09:00, there will be a presentation of the report, which can be followed via webcast.

From Albert, CEO Fredrik Bengtsson and CFO Katarina Strivall will participate. The presentation will be in English and will conclude with a Q&A session.

Webcast link: https://www.finwire.tv/webcast/albert/q1-2025/

data.title: Acerinox falls after Q1 net profit slumps on weak steel demand
data.description:
** Shares of Acerinox fall 3.9% after its Q1 net profit slumped as tariff-related uncertainties and an adverse backdrop caused markets to slow down and postponed an awaited demand recovery

** The Spanish steelmaker books a net profit of 10 million euros ($11.28 million) in Q1, down 81% from a year ago

** The company also flags concerns that production that previously supplied North America would now flood the EU and hurt its business there

** The shares are at the bottom of Madrid's IBEX 35 index, which falls 0.2%

** Including Thursday's fall, the stock has gained 4.6% YTD​

($1 = 0.8863 euros)

data.title: PREVIEW-India's Titan rises ahead of fourth-quarter results
data.description:
** Shares of jeweler and watchmaker Titan Company rise 1.7% ahead of results

** Analysts expect the company's consolidated revenue to rise nearly 26%, boosted by robust sale of higher price jewelry and gold coins

** Titan reported jewelry business growth of 25% in a Q4 business update, topping expectations of analysts at J.P. Morgan and Axis Capital

** TITN shares fell about 6% in the January-to-March quarter on worries that high gold prices would dent demand, per analysts

** Titan gets a bulk of revenue from affluent Indians, whose purchasing power and inclination to indulge is unaffected by inflation and higher rates, co's finance chief Ashok Sonthalia told Reuters in February

** Titan is rated 'buy' on avg by analysts, compared to avg rating of 'strong buy' on peer Kalyan Jewellers India - data compiled by LSEG

** TITN shares up 4.19% in the last 12 months, including session's gains

data.title: Indian benchmarks flat as US growth, Indo-Pak worries offset trade optimism
data.description:
** Indian benchmarks NSE Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex trade flat as investors weigh tensions with Pakistan and worries over U.S. economy

** India hit Pakistan and Pakistani Kashmir with missiles on Wednesday, Pakistan vowed to retaliate

** Caution over border tensions, worries over U.S. economy, as flagged by Fed, are offsetting global trade optimism, analysts say

** India and UK signed a trade deal on Tuesday; UK is also reportedly close to a trade deal with the U.S.

** U.S. and China are scheduled for trade talks this weekend

** Eight of the 13 major Indian sectors decline; defence stocks (.NIFTYINDDEFENCE) rise 1.5%

** Small- and mid-caps rise 1% and 0.25%

* Tata Motors , which makes luxury Jaguar Land Rover cars in Britain, gains 2% after reports of imminent UK-U.S. trade deal

data.title: OpenAI Hires Instacart CEO as Head of Applications
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

OpenAI said Instacart's chief executive is joining the company to lead its applications business as the ChatGPT maker looks to scale its operations and drive growth.

Sam Altman, who will remain as OpenAI's CEO, said in a statement Wednesday that Fidji Simo will join later this year and report to him.

"Applications brings together a group of existing business and operational teams responsible for how our research reaches and benefits the world," Altman said. Simo will "focus on enabling our 'traditional' company functions to scale as we enter a next phase of growth," he said.

In this new configuration, "I'll be able to increase my focus on research, compute, and safety," Altman said in a post on X. "These are critical as we approach superintelligence."

Simo has served as an OpenAI board member over the past year. In a post on X, Simo said she will remain CEO of Instacart for the next few months and board chair afterward.

Simo joined Instacart in 2021 as its CEO after spending a decade at Meta Platforms' Facebook. She took the grocery-delivery company public in 2023, expanding its core business while diving into other areas, such as advertising.

In an emailed statement, Instacart said it expects the company's soon-to-be-announced new CEO "will be one of its highly talented senior executives."

News Corp, owner of The Wall Street Journal and Dow Jones Newswires, has a content-licensing partnership with OpenAI.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Foreign tourist arrivals to Bosnia rise 3.4% y/y in March
data.description:
SARAJEVO (Bosnia and Herzegovina), May 8 (SeeNews) - The number of foreign tourists who visited Bosnia and Herzegovina reached 66,471 in March, up 3.4% on the year and 8.2% higher on the month, the statistics office said.

In March, foreign tourists spent 142,603 nights in the country, down 0.7% on the same month last year, Bosnia's Agency for Statistics said in a monthly report on Wednesday.

The total number of tourists who visited Bosnia in March decreased 5.8% on the year, to 96,896. The total number of overnights went down 5.6% to 206,088.

In March, most foreign visitors to Bosnia came from Croatia (15,128), Serbia (10,325) and Turkey (6,765). The number of visitors from Turkey rose 53.8% year-on-year in March.

data.title: Spanish Annual Industrial Output Up 1% in March
data.description:
Spain's industrial production, adjusted for seasonal and calendar effects, climbed 1% year over year in March, against the 1.9% fall in February, provisional data from the National Statistics Institute showed Thursday.

On a monthly basis, Spanish industrial output rose 0.9%, against the prior 0.7% increase.

data.title: Concord Healthcare's Controlling Shareholder Boosts Stake
data.description:
Concord Healthcare's controlling shareholder, Medstar (Shanghai) Enterprise Management, acquired an additional 45,600 H shares of the company for HK$311,904, a Wednesday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The shares, which were purchased from the open market for HK$6.84 apiece, represent roughly 0.01% of the Chinese oncology healthcare service provider's total issued share capital.

data.title: Stressed market conditions in derivatives on SINCH (149/25)
data.description:
NASDAQ NordicProduct information

Stressed market conditions in derivatives on SINCH (149/25)

Nasdaq has decided to call for stressed market conditions in derivatives onSinch AB (SINCH) for four hours, after which time an assessment of marketconditions will be made to determine if stressed market conditions are stillvalid.

Stressed market conditions in derivatives is called for when the underlyingasset to a derivative experience high and short term intraday volatility orwhen other factors causes high uncertainty in the pricing of derivatives. Whenstressed market is called for it means that market makers are allowed to quoteprices with double the normal spread.

For contact details please see attached file.https://view.news.eu.nasdaq.com/view?id=b6edc71737983c67b0b8ca90cf8d1a708&lang=en&src=omxlink

data.title: Nama Chemicals' Vice Chair Resigns
data.description:
Nama Chemicals' (SASE:2210) Vice Chairman Abdulaziz Abdulrahman Al-Ammar resigned, effective immediately.

Al-Ammar left the role due to working conditions, according to a Thursday release by Saudi Arabia-listed chemical manufacturing group.

data.title: Ukraine, allies discuss ways to pressure Russia into 30-day ceasefire
data.description:
Ukraine has discussed ways to pressure Russia into agreeing to a 30-day ceasefire with U.S., French, British and German senior officials, President Volodymyr Zelenskiy's top aide said on Thursday, part of a flurry of diplomacy to try to end the war.

Andriy Yermak, Zelenskiy's chief of staff, did not say when the online talks with U.S. special envoys, Steve Witkoff and Keith Kellogg, France's top diplomatic adviser Emmanuel Bonne, Britain's national security adviser Jonathan Powell and Guenter Sautter, a top adviser to the German leader, took place.

But he said all parties "emphasised that a complete and unconditional ceasefire should be the first step towards a just and sustainable peace".

"We also discussed instruments of pressure in case Russia refuses the initiative or violates the agreements," Yermak said on Telegram.

Ukraine has expressed readiness to accept a U.S. proposal to enact an immediate, interim 30-day ceasefire, which could be extended by mutual agreement of the parties, and has accused the Kremlin of ignoring the plan. Putin has proposed a ceasefire of only three days - from May 8-10.

"The Kremlin's proposal for a three-day truce is not about peace, but about safely holding of the parade in Moscow. This is political manipulation," Yermak said.

Russia, which launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022, is preparing to stage a military parade on May 9 to mark the 80th anniversary of the defeat of Nazi Germany in what President Vladimir Putin hopes will be a show of strength.

On Thursday, after the declared ceasefire had come into effect, the skies over Ukraine's major cities were quiet, though there were reports of clashes in eastern Ukraine.

data.title: Swedish Krona, Norwegian Krone Fall Ahead of Rate DecisionsMarket Talk
data.description: The Swedish krona and Norwegian krone fall as investors trade with caution ahead of decisions from the two countries' central banks, where both are expected to keep interest rates on hold. Sweden's Riksbank is likely to remain in "wait and see mode" for the foreseeable future, Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. The Norges Bank could reiterate its prior view that rates will likely be cut at some point this year, though it "probably won't pull the trigger" until the second half of 2025, he says. The euro rises 0.1% to 10.9393 Swedish krona and 0.3% to 11.7298 Norwegian krone. The Riksbank decision is at 0730 GMT and the Norges Bank's decision is at 0800 GMT. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: REG - Hikma Pharmaceutical - Settlement agreement for Xyrem® US lawsuits
data.description:
RNS Number : 8679H Hikma Pharmaceuticals Plc 08 May 2025  

Hikma announces settlement agreement for Xyrem® (sodium oxybate) U.S. class action lawsuits

·    Hikma has entered into a preliminary class settlement agreement that resolves the majority of its Xyrem® (sodium oxybate) antitrust class action cases in the US

·    Under the agreement and subject to court approval, Hikma would pay up to $50 million in cash

London, 8 May 2025 - Hikma Pharmaceuticals PLC, along with its wholly owned subsidiary Hikma Pharmaceuticals USA Inc. (the Company), today announced that it has reached an agreement to resolve the majority of the class action antitrust lawsuits brought against the Company by third-party payors in the US who have purchased or been billed for Xyrem® (sodium oxybate).  These matters have been previously disclosed as contingent liabilities in our financial disclosures.

"We are pleased to have reached a settlement agreement that protects the Company's interests and provides clarity to our stakeholders," said Sam Park, Hikma's General Counsel. "We are proud to have facilitated patients' access to a lower-cost authorised generic version of this essential medicine, years ahead of its patent expiration."

The settlement is subject to court approval and resolves the majority of the cases pending against Hikma. If all conditions are satisfied, Hikma agrees to pay up to $50 million in cash.

This settlement is not an admission of wrongdoing or liability, and Hikma will continue to defend itself vigorously against any litigation that this settlement does not resolve.

- ENDS -

About Hikma


(NASDAQ Dubai: HIK) (rated BBB-/stable S&P and BBB-/positive Fitch)

Hikma helps put better health within reach every day for millions of people around the world. For more than 45 years, we've been creating high-quality medicines and making them accessible to the people who need them. Headquartered in the UK, we are a global company with a local presence across North America, the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) and Europe, and we use our unique insight and expertise to transform cutting-edge science into innovative solutions that transform people's lives. We're committed to our customers, and the people they care for, and by thinking creatively and acting practically, we provide them with a broad range of branded and non-branded generic medicines. Together, our 9,500 colleagues are helping to shape a healthier world that enriches all our communities. We are a leading licensing partner, and through our venture capital arm, are helping bring innovative health technologies to people around the world. For more information, please visit: www.hikma.com

data.title: South Korean Stocks Rise for 3rd Day
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI rose 0.2% to close at 2,579 on Thursday, marking its third straight session of gains and holding near six-week highs, led by battery stocks.

The index also tracked Wall Street’s overnight gains after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged.

Meanwhile, investors remained cautious ahead of US-China trade talks in Switzerland, as President Trump ruled out easing tariffs before the meeting, keeping expectations low.

At home, the government plans to execute 70% of the 13.8 trillion-won extra budget within three months to swiftly support the economy and exporters.

South Korea’s finance minister also said authorities would closely monitor financial and foreign exchange markets amid ongoing trade negotiations and geopolitical tensions.

Among battery makers, LG Energy Solution climbed 1.6%, while Samsung SDI adding 0.4%.

Notable gains extended to shipbuilding stocks, including Hyundai Heavy Industries (1.7%) and Korea Shipbuilding & Offshore Engineering (2.5%).

data.title: Some U.K. Stocks Are Undervalued, Likely to Outperform PeersMarket Talk
data.description: U.K. financials, cyclicals, and domestic stocks have cheap valuations relative to their U.S. peers, and are likely to perform better than their counterparts, Clive Beagles, senior fund manager at JO Hambro says in a note. However, U.K. defensive stocks such as Bunzl have expensive valuations following years of outperformance, he says. Overall, the U.K. market is "cheap compared to the rest of the world and other major markets," Beagles says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)
